item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really cool, but I spent the whole description of the animal and plant life confused and wondering if the robot was making them up, as it had seemingly been stated earlier that there was only patches of fungus.

I think this confusion is a result of the word "but" being removed from that line! It changes the whole meaning of the sentence! If you have some objection to the word "but", it should have been changed to "however" or something, not simply removed IMO.

That said, if the animals are real … why haven't the researchers had one of the robots bring them in front of the camera?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I realize I'm, what, 5 years late to the party, but I stumbled across the ORIA Hub this weekend, and this is probably my favorite article from that hub. So well written, the art is FANTASTIC, and I really got hit right in the feels by the idea of the robots trying to make sense of the world they were seeing, using information and language from an intelligence not of that world… Well done!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Those are some of the best pictures I have seen in any SCP article, also really interesting in general.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This made me think about how I'd handle the existential dread of being one of the robots. Beautifully melancholy ending. +1

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

This article is great, and largely self-contained. There are not many other articles on the site I feel I could make a link to without it feeling really shoehorned in. That being said, there are mentions of ORIA which can inline link to the GoI hub, and GRU-P, which can link to The Coldest War since this latter group lacks a hub proper and that canon is a great use of the GoI on the site. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

A joint research facility has been constructed around the cave housing SCP-1411-A. It will be manned at all times by agents from both the Foundation and the Office for the Reclamation of Islamic Artifacts. Colonel Khorsandi of the ORIA is in charge of security, as well as liasing with the government of Uzbekistan. Doctor Yi will supervise all examination of SCP-1411-A.

To this:

A joint research facility has been constructed around the cave housing SCP-1411-A. It will be manned at all times by agents from both the Foundation and the Office for the Reclamation of Islamic Artifacts. Colonel Khorsandi of the ORIA is in charge of security, as well as liasing with the government of Uzbekistan. Doctor Yi will supervise all examination of SCP-1411-A.

Changing this:

SCP-1411-A was discovered by members of the Uzbek military after its anomalous properties began manifesting in region. GRU Division P was initially contacted, but a deterioration in relations between the Uzbekistan and Russian governments led to the ORIA being brought in instead. The ORIA then requested assistance from the Foundation as the anomalies began to intensify.

To this:

SCP-1411-A was discovered by members of the Uzbek military after its anomalous properties began manifesting in region. GRU Division P was initially contacted, but a deterioration in relations between the Uzbekistan and Russian governments led to the ORIA being brought in instead. The ORIA then requested assistance from the Foundation as the anomalies began to intensify.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It reminded me of Moon. I really liked that movie, and I really like this scip too.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really not digging this line:

No animal life has been observed through the feed (but see Document SCP-1411-3).

The "but" is really not clinical. However, I've read this before, and I liked it then, so, you know, upboats.

Edit: Also, I'm unsure about the grammar in this one:

…(assuming the components of SCP-1411-B is similar in size to SCP-1411-A)…

Crap. Am I about to do a line-by-line?

Edit 2: Yep.

+Line-by-Line
-Why do I do this to myself?

GRU Division P was initially contacted, but a deterioration in relations between the Uzbekistan and Russian governments led to the ORIA being brought in instead.

This ain't clinical. It delivers the message, yes, but it doesn't read like an internal memo from the Men-in-Black on the nature, maintenance, and history of a dangerous object. Maybe "SCP-1411 was initially assessed by GRU Division P. However, deterioration in international relations between Uzbekistan and the Russian Federation lead to the Office for the Reclamation of Islamic Artifacts being tasked with responsibility for containment of the anomaly."
Wordy? Yes, but also more clinical. CLINICAL DOCUMENTS FOR THE BUREAUCRAT GOD! STAPLERS FOR THE STAPLER THRONE!

Activations ceased for several days until the anomalies began to manifest again.

This isn't clinical; "until anomalous activity remanifested," maybe?

On 13 June, 2014, Agent Durani was injured by a fall near the local site. She was rushed to a nearby hospital.

Tone again. "On 13, June, 2014, Agent Durani was injured while hiking and had to be hospitalized." « My suggestion.

No more quote boxes, but I wish there was a way to reformat those pictures so they wouldn't drop past the final footnote and such.

-Why do I do this to myself?

Anyway, I really like this, despite my line-by-line. MOAR!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awesome. It really filled me with awe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Strange new world and robot feels. *gets all teary-eyed*

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All I can say is that is must be horrible to notice you're inside a robotic body, unable to ever go home… and that you're not even the original.
The first robots, even worse, since they had no idea what happened, how, why, and where they were…

Dying so many times without even knowing what it felt like…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dig the concept, poetry and most of all the illustrations, and the logs written by the robot are a nice touch. Only thing I didn't get was what the implications were that djkaktus was mentioning, could you inform me about them?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is multi-media collaboration at its finest! :-D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You can always make grammatical fixes, so long as they don't change the style and meaning of the document.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Grammar is messy, skip is not. +1, but can I fix the grammar sometime in the near future?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I mentioned this in chat, but this thing is really fucking cool. At its core, it's still just another omg alternate world crazy things happenin but what you've done with the implications of that world are so unique. It was cool to get to help with this, thanks a ton! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very interesting, and the pictures look really cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Art is by Sunny Parallax, with photo manipulation done by djkaktus to dirty 'em up a bit. They're both awesome.

EDIT: I am an idiot and forgot to credit the poetry to Zyn, who is also awesome. In fact, you're awesome too. Everyone's awesome forever.
